info.name: Clan of the Divine Warrior
info.description: The Divine Warrior subclass for the Crusader.
info.author: Guzufu

description.p: Fight valiantly using the power of the divine against your foes

Many Crusader clans have close relationships with divinity, especially those that represent the ideals of civilisation and honourable combat. Many of the skills taught by this clan were first learned from orders of Paladins but they have since been adapted to fit the skillsets of the crusaders that charge forth in the name of the Gods.

You empower your strikes to fight against the evils of the realms. When you make a weapon attack against an abberation, fey, fiend or undead, you can add an additional 1d8 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) to the target.
In addition, your Weak Point damage type is changed from your weapon type to either radiant or necrotic (your choice).

sheet.description: When you make a weapon attack against an abberation, fey, fiend or undead, you can add an additional 1d8 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) to the target. In addition, your Weak Point damage type is changed from your weapon type to either radiant or necrotic (your choice).

element.name: Level 7: Empowered Protection

description.p: The power of the divine flows through you and empowers you with the ability to protect and heal your allies. When you critically hit against an enemy, you can choose one ally within 30 feet. The ally regains a number of hit points equal to the Weak Point damage dealt. You can use this feature a number of times per long rest equal to your proficiency bonus.

element.sheet: > When you make a weapon attack against an abberation, fey, fiend or undead, you can add an additional 1d8 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) to the target. In addition, your Weak Point damage type is changed from your weapon type to either radiant or necrotic (your choice).

description.p: When you activate your Crusader Ward, all creatures within 30 feet who have less than half of their hit points regain hit points equal to 1d6 plus your Crusader level.

sheet.description: When you activate your Crusader Ward, all creatures within 30 feet who have less than half of their hit points regain {{crusader:level}} hit points.

element.name: Level 15: Divine Retribution

description.p: You are a paragon of defending the divine and your faith as well as your allies. When a creature takes damage within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to force the creature that dealt the damage to make a wisdom saving throw. Charisma is used as the basis of the DC of this save. On a failed save, the creature takes radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) equal to the amount dealt and on a successful save, the creature takes half damage. Once you have used this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

element.sheet: >> When a creature takes damage within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to force the creature that dealt the damage to make a DC {{divine retribution:save}} wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) equal to the amount dealt and on a successful save, the creature takes half damage.
